title: Pediomelum cyphocalyx
taxonomy: Pediomelum cyphocalyx is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Pediomelum. Pediomelum cyphocalyx is native to the southwestern United States. The plant is also known as the "twisted-ace" or "twisted locoweed". It is a perennial herb that grows in dry to moist areas.

intro: Pediomelum cyphocalyx, also known as the rocky mountain pediomelum or Utah pediomelum, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of western North America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems, due to its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
